strands.title: PR #1 Closed: File Manifest
strands.content:
PR #1 is effectively closed. The complete file manifest is settled:

Created: auth/exceptions.py (OAuthOnlyAccount), auth/oauth.py (exchange_code, fetch_user_info, upsert_oauth_user, OAuthError, _raise_for_provider_error), migrations/versions/XXXX_add_user_oauth_accounts.py.

Modified: models.py (OAuthIdentity model), decorators.py (Bug 1 fix), api/routes.py (Bug 2 fix), app.py (two OAuth routes).

Tests created: test_decorators.py, test_oauth.py, test_login.py, one integration test.

Not in PR #1: auth/cache.py, auth/tokens.py, test_cache.py.

key_claims.text: PR #1 file manifest is complete and settled
auth/cache.py, auth/tokens.py, test_cache.py are NOT in PR #1

strands.title: Security Fixes (Shipped in PR #1)
strands.content: Both security fixes shipped in PR #1. Bug 1: decorators.py — abort(403) when is_active is False. Bug 2: api/routes.py — abort(401) when key.user.is_active is False. Both were one-line fixes, independent of all refactor work. Regression coverage in test_decorators.py.

key_claims.text: Bug 1 and Bug 2: both shipped, both regression-tested

strands.title: OAuthIdentity Model & Migration (Shipped in PR #1)
strands.content: Table: user_oauth_accounts. Columns: id (PK), user_id (FK→users.id CASCADE), provider (String 50), provider_user_id (String 256), provider_email (String 256, nullable), created_at (DateTime tz, server_default now()), last_used_at (DateTime tz, nullable). Two UniqueConstraints: uq_oauth_user_provider(user_id, provider); uq_oauth_provider_identity(provider, provider_user_id). Index: ix_oauth_identity_user_id. All constraint names explicit. FK: ondelete=CASCADE, name=fk_oauth_accounts_user_id. ORM: lazy='raise' on both sides; cascade='all, delete-orphan'. Migration downgrade(): drop_index with if_exists=True, then drop_table. Shipped in PR #1.

key_claims.text: OAuthIdentity model and migration shipped in PR #1
lazy='raise' on both sides — joinedload(OAuthIdentity.user) REQUIRED in all callers

strands.title: PR #2 Scope: Caching + Tokens
strands.content:
PR #2 contains: (a) auth/cache.py — get_cached_api_key_user, set_cached_api_key_user, invalidate_api_key; wired into decorators.py but inactive until Redis is configured. invalidate_all_keys_for_user raises NotImplementedError (reverse index deferred). (b) auth/tokens.py — lookup_api_key extraction (SRP fix). (c) test_cache.py — Redis failure swallowed silently; cache miss falls back to DB. (d) Full auth/ consolidation remains deferred indefinitely.

Key cache invariants: function-based in auth/cache.py (not inline), sha256(raw_key) as cache key, TTL 300s (configurable), auth:apk:* prefix on shared Redis instance, reuse existing client. Invalidate on ApiKey.delete() and User.is_active flip. is_active flip invalidation: accept 300s propagation delay unless immediate revocation is a security requirement (open question).

allkeys-lru risk: cache miss is a DB hit, not a failure — flag to ops.

key_claims.text: auth/cache.py is function-based, not inline — code review requirement
Share existing Redis with job queue — auth:apk:* prefix, reuse existing client
invalidate_all_keys_for_user raises NotImplementedError — reverse index deferred
Cache wired but inactive until Redis configured — no new infra in PR #2

strands.title: OAuth Behavioral Invariants (Shipped in PR #1)
strands.content:
Core OAuth logic shipped in auth/oauth.py and app.py. Behavioral invariants:
- provider_user_id (not email) is stable lookup key
- state token validated with hmac.compare_digest, popped immediately after use
- no refresh tokens stored; access token discarded after user info fetch
- _raise_for_provider_error omits resp.text
- Google: email_verified=False sets email=None
- GitHub: /user/emails soft-fails to None
- Email-merge safe for Google/GitHub (both verify emails)
- upsert_oauth_user lookup priority: OAuthIdentity(provider, provider_user_id) first, then User.email match if email not None
- OAuthOnlyAccount: password_hash is None checked before bcrypt; flash message does not name specific provider
- Reverse case (password user hits OAuth): handled by email-merge in upsert_oauth_user, no extra code

key_claims.text: All OAuth behavioral invariants shipped and tested in PR #1
OAuthOnlyAccount flash message must not name specific provider
Zero new dependencies — requests (existing) + stdlib sufficient

strands.title: Refactor: Deferred Indefinitely
strands.content: Full auth/ consolidation deferred — not cancelled. Trigger conditions: auth sprawl causes bugs, onboarding friction increases, or a third auth method is added. AuthRequest/AuthResult, AnonymousIdentity, g.identity: do NOT implement. auth/tokens.py (lookup_api_key extraction) is the only narrow refactor in PR #2 scope, and only if service.py SRP fix is needed.

key_claims.text: Full auth/ refactor deferred — trigger conditions documented
AuthRequest/AuthResult, AnonymousIdentity, g.identity: do NOT build

open_questions: Does the existing users table use 'users' as its tablename? (Assumed yes in FK — migration will fail if different)
Email-merge on OAuth: is automatic account linking on email match the desired policy, or should it require explicit user confirmation?
What is the current maxmemory-policy on the shared Redis instance? (Determines whether to flag eviction risk urgently to ops)
Is immediate API key revocation a security requirement? (Determines whether TTL=300s propagation delay is acceptable or TTL must be reduced to ~60s)
Does auth/service.py exist in the pre-existing codebase? (Determines whether auth/tokens.py SRP extraction is needed in PR #2)
instructions_for_next:
PR #1 is closed. PR #2 scope is: auth/cache.py (function-based, sha256 key, TTL 300s, auth:apk:* prefix, Redis failure swallowed, invalidate_all_keys_for_user raises NotImplementedError), auth/tokens.py (lookup_api_key, only if service.py exists), test_cache.py (Redis failure + cache miss fallback), wire cache into decorators.py (inactive until Redis configured).

Full auth/ consolidation remains deferred indefinitely.

Key invariants to carry: joinedload(OAuthIdentity.user) required everywhere, cache is function-based not inline, share existing Redis client, do NOT build AuthRequest/AuthResult/AnonymousIdentity/g.identity, do NOT warm cache.

declared_losses.why: Test coverage is now fully captured in the PR #1 manifest and PR #2 scope strands. A dedicated test strand was redundant — the information integrates cleanly into the strands it belongs to.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: OAuth+Password Clash as a standalone strand
declared_losses.why: The design is settled and shipped in PR #1. Its key claims (OAuthOnlyAccount exception, message policy, check order, reverse case) are fully integrated into 'OAuth Behavioral Invariants (Shipped in PR #1)'. No unresolved questions remain.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: OAuth2 Bolt-On (Option A) as a standalone strand
declared_losses.why: PR #1 is closed. Option A is no longer a recommendation under consideration — it was built. The scope, line estimates, and decision rationale no longer need to be carried. Invariants that matter going forward are in 'OAuth Behavioral Invariants (Shipped in PR #1)'.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Caching: Settled Design as a standalone strand (full detail)
declared_losses.why: Collapsed into 'PR #2 Scope: Caching + Tokens'. Cache design is settled; the strand was carrying deliberation and options that no longer need to be active (e.g. the three is_active-flip options). The resolution (option 2, 300s TTL) is carried forward.

feedback_to_harness: <parameter name="process_observations">["PR closure is a natural consolidation trigger — dropped 4 strands (OAuth2 Bolt-On, OAuth+Password Clash, Testing Strategy, Caching standalone) and rebuilt 6 tighter ones. This felt like the right moment to compress: deliberation strands become fact strands once decisions ship.", "Harness feedback to reduce strand count was timely — the PR summary turn gave a natural forcing function to act on it. Down from 7 to 6 strands, with higher density per strand."]
